data.title: Sunoco LP SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Sunoco LP, a leading distributor of motor fuels and operator of convenience stores, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and operational activities, reflecting both challenges and strategic initiatives undertaken during the period.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenues: Sunoco LP reported total revenues of $5,390 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, a decrease from $6,174 million in the same period in 2024. This decline was primarily due to lower sales revenue.

Gross Profit: The company achieved a gross profit of $416 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $488 million in the same period in 2024. The decrease was driven by lower segment profits.

Operating Income: Operating income rose to $203 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, up from $150 million in the same period in 2024. This increase was attributed to higher Adjusted EBITDA despite increased depreciation and amortization.

Net Income: Net income for the three months ended June 30, 2025, was $86 million, down from $501 million in the same period in 2024. The significant decrease was largely due to the absence of a $598 million gain from the West Texas Sale recorded in 2024.

Net Income Attributable to Partners: Net income attributable to partners was $86 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $493 million in the same period in 2024.

Basic Net Income Per Common Unit: Basic net income per common unit was $0.33 for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $3.88 in the same period in 2024, reflecting the decrease in net income.

Diluted Net Income Per Common Unit: Diluted net income per common unit was $0.33 for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $3.85 in the same period in 2024.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The Fuel Distribution segment reported a decrease in segment profit due to lower profit per gallon and a decrease in gallons sold, primarily attributed to the West Texas Sale. Conversely, the Pipeline Systems segment saw an increase in segment profit due to the acquisition of NuStar and the formation of ET-S Permian. The Terminals segment experienced an increase in segment profit due to the acquisition of NuStar and Zenith European terminals.

Geographical Performance: Sunoco LP operates in over 40 U.S. states, Puerto Rico, Europe, and Mexico. The acquisition of TanQuid, which owns fuel terminals in Germany and Poland, is expected to enhance the company's presence in Europe.

Sales Units: In the Fuel Distribution segment, motor fuel gallons sold were slightly down, with 2,188 million gallons sold in Q2 2025 compared to 2,189 million gallons in Q2 2024. The Pipeline Systems segment reported a decrease in throughput volumes due to the contribution of assets to ET-S Permian.

New Production Launches: The company announced the acquisition of TanQuid, which includes 15 fuel terminals in Germany and one in Poland, expected to close in the second half of 2025.

Future Outlook: The Parkland Acquisition is expected to close in Q4 2025, which will involve the creation of SunocoCorp LLC, a publicly traded entity. The company anticipates that the One Big Beautiful Bill Act will defer the payment of a significant portion of its corporate subsidiaries’ U.S. federal income taxes in future periods.

SEC Filing: Sunoco LP [ SUN ] - 10-Q - Aug. 07, 2025

data.title: CHEMUNG FINANCIAL CORP SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Chemung Financial Corp, a financial services company primarily engaged in commercial, residential, and consumer lending, as well as wealth management services, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and operational highlights for the period ending June 30, 2025.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Interest Income: $20.8 million, increased by 17.2% compared to the same period in the prior year, primarily due to higher interest income on loans and lower interest expense on deposits.
  • Net Income (Loss): $(6.5) million, a decrease from $5.0 million in the same period of the prior year, primarily due to a $17.5 million net loss on securities transactions.
  • Basic and Diluted Earnings (Loss) Per Share: $(1.35), compared to $1.05 in the same period of the prior year, reflecting the impact of the net loss on securities transactions.
  • Net Interest Margin, Fully Taxable Equivalent: 3.05%, an increase from 2.66% in the same period of the prior year, driven by a higher yield on interest-earning assets and a lower cost of interest-bearing liabilities.
  • Provision for Credit Losses: $1.1 million, increased by 30.3% compared to the same period in the prior year, due to stronger loan growth and changes in economic forecasts.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The Corporation's revenue is primarily derived from interest and fees on loans, interest income on investment securities, and fees from wealth management services. The Bank's income is largely generated from commercial, residential, and consumer loans, as well as wealth management services.
  • Geographical Performance: The Corporation's commercial lending demand remains strong across its footprint, particularly in the Capital Bank and Canal Bank divisions in the Albany and Western New York markets. The Canal Bank division has seen accelerated loan growth in 2025 following the opening of a regional banking center and expansion of the commercial lending team.
  • Sales Units: The Corporation originated $22.2 million in residential mortgages during the six months ended June 30, 2025, with $4.9 million sold in the secondary market. The commercial mortgage portfolio increased by $80.5 million, while indirect consumer loans decreased by $24.8 million.
  • New Production Launches: The Corporation expanded its operations in the Western New York market with the opening of a regional banking center, which contributed to accelerated loan growth in the Canal Bank division.
  • Future Outlook: Management anticipates using proceeds from the issuance of subordinated debt and sale of available for sale securities to fund organic loan growth across the Corporation's footprint in future periods. The issuance of subordinated debt is expected to strengthen the Corporation's regulatory capital position and improve commercial real estate concentration ratios, allowing for flexibility in pursuing loan growth in key expansion markets.

SEC Filing: CHEMUNG FINANCIAL CORP [ CHMG ] - 10-Q - Aug. 07, 2025

data.title: AMERICAN FINANCIAL GROUP INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
American Financial Group Inc. (AFG), a prominent player in the insurance industry, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial and operational performance, highlighting both the challenges and achievements faced during the period.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenues: $3,780 million, reflecting a slight decrease compared to the same period last year, indicating stable performance despite market challenges.
  • Net Earnings: $328 million, showing a decrease from the previous year, primarily due to increased losses and loss adjustment expenses.
  • Earnings per Common Share: $3.92, down from $5.38 in the prior year, reflecting the impact of reduced net earnings.
  • Total Basic Earnings: $3.92 per share, consistent with diluted earnings, indicating no significant impact from potential share dilution.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The Property and Casualty Insurance segment is divided into three sub-segments: Property and Transportation, Specialty Casualty, and Specialty Financial. The Property and Transportation sub-segment includes physical damage and liability coverage for buses and trucks, inland and ocean marine, and agricultural-related products. Specialty Casualty covers excess and surplus, executive and professional liability, and workers’ compensation insurance. Specialty Financial includes risk management insurance programs for lending and leasing institutions, fidelity and surety products, and trade credit insurance.
  • Geographical Performance: Sales of property and casualty insurance outside of the United States represented 4% of AFG’s revenues in both the second quarter and first six months of 2025 and 2024, indicating a consistent contribution from international markets.
  • Operational Performance: The Property and Casualty Insurance segment reported a total underwriting profit of $113 million for the second quarter of 2025, with the Specialty Casualty sub-segment contributing the highest underwriting profit of $49 million.
  • Future Outlook: The Chief Operating Decision Makers (CODMs) evaluate the performance of the Property and Casualty Insurance segment based on return on equity and underwriting profit, which are key metrics for resource allocation and capital decisions. The internal reinsurance results are now included within the same sub-segments as the ceding businesses to better reflect the performance of the underlying operating businesses.
  • Managed Investment Entities: AFG manages and has investments in collateralized loan obligations (CLOs), which are considered variable interest entities. AFG’s maximum exposure to economic loss on the CLOs it manages is limited to its investment in those CLOs, which had an aggregate fair value of $148 million at June 30, 2025.
  • Claims and Reserves: The net decrease in the provision for claims of prior years during the first six months of 2025 reflects lower than anticipated losses in the crop business, lower than anticipated claim severity in the aviation and agribusiness operations, and lower than expected claim frequency in the property and inland marine business.

SEC Filing: AMERICAN FINANCIAL GROUP INC [ AFG ] - 10-Q - Aug. 07, 2025

data.title: Perimeter Solutions Inc reports results for the quarter ended June 30 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Perimeter Solutions Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 39 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended June 30, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 14 cents. The mean expectation of two analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 27 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 25 cents to 28 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 27.8% to $162.64 million from a year ago; analysts expected $139.65 million.

  • Perimeter Solutions Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was a loss of 22 cents​.

  • The company reported a quarterly loss of $32.16 million.

  • Perimeter Solutions Inc shares had risen by 13.6% this quarter and gained 23.8% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 12.8% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no negative revisions of earnings estimates

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 2 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the specialty chemicals peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Perimeter Solutions Inc is $18.50, about 14.5% above its last closing price of $15.82

data.title: Haemonetics Corp reports results for the quarter ended June 30 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Haemonetics Corp reported quarterly adjusted earnings of $1.10​​ per share for the quarter ended June 30, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of $1.02. The mean expectation of eleven analysts for the quarter was for earnings of $1.02 per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 95 cents to $1.10 per share.

  • Revenue fell 4.4% to $321.39 million from a year ago; analysts expected $304.38 million.

  • Haemonetics Corp's reported EPS for the quarter was 70 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $34.03 million.

  • Haemonetics Corp shares had risen by 1.6% this quarter and lost 3.0% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 5.2% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, one analyst negatively revised an earnings estimate

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 10 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and 1 "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the medical equipment, supplies & distribution peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Haemonetics Corp is $92.50, about 18.1% above its last closing price of $75.77

data.title: Nexstar Media Group Inc reports results for the quarter ended June 30 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Nexstar Media Group Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of $3.06​​ per share for the quarter ended June 30, lower than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of $3.54. The mean expectation of seven analysts for the quarter was for earnings of $2.70 per share. Wall Street expected results to range from $1.14 to $3.40 per share.

  • Revenue fell 3.2% to $1.23 billion from a year ago; analysts expected $1.21 billion.

  • Nexstar Media Group Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was $3.18​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $97 million.

  • Nexstar Media Group Inc shares had risen by 8.1% this quarter and gained 18.4% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 7.9% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 7 "strong buy" or "buy," 2 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the broadcasting peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Nexstar Media Group Inc is $200.00, about 6.5% above its last closing price of $186.99

data.title: REG - Metlen Energy&Metals - Right of Squeeze-Out
data.description:
RNS Number : 4899U Metlen Energy & Metals PLC 07 August 2025  

ANNOUNCEMENT

APPROVAL OF THE METLEN ENERGY & METALS PLC REQUEST TO COMPULSORILY ACQUIRE THE REMAINING ORDINARY REGISTERED SHARES OF METLEN ENERGY & METALS S.A.

07.08.2025

1.            In accordance with Article 2 of the decision 1/644/22.4.2013 ("Decision 1/644") of the Board of Directors of the Hellenic Capital Market Commission ("HCMC"), Metlen Energy & Metals PLC ("Metlen PLC") announces that, on 07.08.2025, the Board of Directors of the HCMC approved Metlen PLC's written request made on 04.08.2025 in relation to the exercise of its right pursuant to Article 27 of Law 3461/2006 (the "Law") to compulsorily acquire (the "Right of Squeeze-Out") the remaining ordinary registered shares, each having a nominal value of €0.97 (each a "Metlen S.A. Share") issued by Metlen Energy & Metals S.A. ("Metlen S.A."), which Metlen PLC has not acquired through the voluntary share exchange tender offer it submitted on 25.06.2025 in accordance with the Law (the "Tender Offer"), in consideration for newly issued Euro denominated ordinary registered shares, each having a nominal value of €11.00 issuable by Metlen PLC (the "Consideration Shares").

The above approval of the Board of Directors of the HCMC was notified to Metlen PLC on 07.08.2025, while 25.08.2025 was set as the date on which trading of the Metlen S.A. Shares on the Athens Exchange ("ATHEX") will cease. As of the same date, the ability to exercise the sell-out right pursuant to Article 28 of the Law will also cease, due to the end of trading of the Metlen S.A. Shares.

2.            At the end of the trading session on the ATHEX on 07.08.2025, the Right of Squeeze-Out relates to the acquisition of 14,083,937 Metlen S.A. Shares (the "Relevant Shares"), corresponding to c. 9.84% of the total share capital and voting rights of Metlen S.A., in consideration for, at the election of the relevant holder of Relevant Shares, either one Consideration Share for each Relevant Share, or payment of €39.62 in cash per Relevant Share (the "Cash Alternative").

It is noted that Metlen PLC will assume payment of the clearing duties in favour of the Hellenic Central Securities Depositary S.A., which would otherwise be payable by the holders of Relevant Shares.

3.            The following are noted in connection with the Right of Squeeze-Out:

(a)

In the context of the Right of Squeeze-Out, the holders of Relevant Shares will be entitled to elect to receive Consideration Shares in book-entry form through either CREST or the Greek Dematerialised Securities System ("DSS").

(b)

In the context of the Right of Squeeze-Out, the holders of Relevant Shares will be entitled to elect to receive Consideration Shares in book-entry form through either CREST or the Greek Dematerialised Securities System ("DSS").

(c)

To be able to elect to receive either Consideration Shares or the Cash Alternative pursuant to the Right of Squeeze-Out, the holders of Relevant Shares should promptly instruct and authorise their participant/intermediary at the DSS (each a "DSS Participant/Intermediary") to proceed with all necessary actions to receive on their behalf the type of consideration, which they will have elected at the Right of Squeeze-Out process. A relevant election form will be made available to the holders of Relevant Shares through the DSS Participants/Intermediaries.

(d)

If, in the context of the Right of Squeeze-out, a holder of Relevant Shares:

(i)

Ηas not elected to receive the Cash Alternative or Consideration Shares through either CREST or the DSS, such holder shall receive Consideration Shares, which will be initially credited to the securities account of the Greek Consignments Deposits and Loans Fund at the DSS.

(ii)

Ηas elected to receive Consideration Shares at CREST but failed to either make promptly the necessary arrangements with such holder's participant at CREST to receive such shares through CREST, or to provide or to timely provide the required information or documents to be able to do so, or such information or documents are erroneous or incomplete, such holder will initially receive Consideration Shares in certificated form.

Important Notices

No person receiving a copy of this announcement or of any other document or material relevant to the Tender Offer or the Right of Squeeze-Out (the "Relevant Documents") in any jurisdiction outside the Hellenic Republic may treat any such document as if it constituted a solicitation or offer to such person and under no circumstances may such person use any Relevant Document if, in the relevant jurisdiction, such solicitation or offer may not be lawfully made to such person or if such Relevant Document may not be lawfully used without breaching any legal requirements. In those instances, any such Relevant Document is sent for information purposes only.

This regulatory announcement does not contain, constitute or form part of any offer or invitation to sell or subscribe or any solicitation of any offer to purchase or subscribe for any securities in any jurisdiction, and neither this regulatory announcement (nor any part of it) nor the fact of its distribution form the basis of, or may be relied upon in connection with, or act as any inducement to enter into, any contract or commitment whatsoever.

data.title: US Stocks Erase Gains
data.description:
US stocks erased early gains to trade mixed on Thursday as markets continued assessed fresh pivots to economic policy by the White House.

The S&P 500 was flat and the Dow lost over 200 points, although another strong session for tech drove the Nasdaq 100 to gain 0.5% for a new record high.

President Trump announced a 100% tariff on imports of semiconductors, although companies that build a portion of their goods domestically or signal future investment could be exempted from the levies.

Nvidia and Broadcom gained 1%, while AMD soared 6% to fully erase its earning-fueled loss from the prior session.

Also, Apple extended its strong momentum from the week with a 3% jump after signaling it would invest $100 billion in US manufacturing.

On the other hand, Eli Lilly plunged 15% after the company released disappointing study results for its weight-loss pill.

Also, Intel fell 2% after President Trump publicly called for the resignation of its CEO.

data.title: Ellison outlines vision for new Paramount with focus on content and technology
data.description:
Key points:
  • Ellison plans to reorganize Paramount into three business units
  • To integrate technology to amplify creativity, not replace it
  • Ellison acknowledges CBS News journalists

Paramount's new chairman and chief executive, David Ellison, outlined a plan to transform the century-old entertainment company into a technology-driven media enterprise in an open letter marking the completion of the merger with Skydance Media.

Ellison said the company would work to transform Paramount by investing in growth businesses that are anchored in its storytelling, working to scale its streaming business globally, and reorganizing the company to make it more efficient.

"We are in the midst of a generational change in our industry - and we understand Paramount has faced its own significant challenges, compounded by the reality of a merger process that stretched out over a considerable time period," Ellison wrote.

"But that time of uncertainty is now behind us. Moving forward, we will work with conviction and optimism to transform Paramount into a tech-forward company that blends the creative heart of Hollywood with the innovative spirit of Silicon Valley."

Paramount Global and Skydance Media Thursday completed their $8.4 billion merger that was announced more than a year ago, capping a long drawn-out deal process marked by political scrutiny and shareholder concerns.

Ellison said he plans to reorganize Paramount into three business units - studios, direct-to-consumer and TV media - to speed decision-making. He plans to move the company onto a single technology platform to reduce costs, and said the company would find efficiencies associated with labor, real estate and procurement, with the goal of achieving a previously announced $2 billion in cost savings.

New Paramount will direct resources "to what matters most," Ellison wrote, delivering more movies, television series, sports, news and games to global audiences.

Paramount will harness technology, not as a replacement for human creativity, but as a tool to amplify creativity.

"From virtual production stages that unleash filmmakers’ limitless imaginations, to AI‑assisted localization that brings shows to new language markets overnight, to a proprietary ad‑tech stack that maximizes yield across streaming and linear platforms, we will thoughtfully integrate these tools into every aspect of our work," Ellison wrote.

Ellison said the company would prioritize growing its streaming business, increasing investment in high-quality exclusive content, which he called the "single biggest driver of subscriber growth."

Sports is a key component of the streaming strategy, as a tool to retain subscribers and keep them watching.

The company plans to move its subscription streaming service, Paramount+, and its free ad-supported PlutoTV service onto the same technology, to help save money and improve the consumer experience.

Ellison laid out the company's core commitments, including to its embattled CBS News division. He acknowledged the journalists' "unwavering commitment" to the news business, and added: "We take immense pride in CBS News’ legacy of impactful journalism and look forward to continuing to foster a newsroom culture where journalists are empowered, trusted, and equipped to do their best work."

In its effort to gain regulatory approval of the merger, Skydance told the Federal Communications Commission that it would appoint an ombudsman to evaluate complaints of editorial bias or other concerns about CBS. It also offered assurances that they were committed to unbiased journalism.

Democrat Anna Gomez, the FCC's dissenter, accused Paramount of "cowardly capitulation" to the Trump administration. She also said the FCC was imposing "never-before-seen controls over newsroom decisions."

data.title: Tech, Media & Telecom Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Technology, Media and Telecom. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1136 ET - President Trump's 100% tariff on semiconductors appears intimidating, but it could have limited consequence, given the exception for companies that commit to manufacturing in the U.S., says Bank of America analyst Vivek Arya. Many of the largest semiconductor companies including TSMC, Samsung and Intel have already committed to ramping up manufacturing in the U.S. Other companies, including Advanced Energy Industries and Texas Instruments, could see an initial hit, but could easily shift more manufacturing to the U.S. "Semis vendors have already started adjusting for the new normal, and impact on profitability could likely be limited over time," Arya says. (nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

0650 ET - Deutsche Telekom's overall results were solid, UBS analysts Polo Tang and Dhruva Kusa Shah write in a note. Despite a competitive German mobile market, the telecommunications company continues to outperform, they say. The group raised its full-year targets for adjusted Ebitda and free cash flow after leases, driven by strong operational results at T-Mobile U.S., they note. The U.S. bank reiterates its view that the group could see 5%-10% mid-term earnings per share and free cash flow upgrades, supported by lower taxes and completion of announced M&A in the U.S., as well as German fiscal stimulus. Shares are down 3.7% at 30.14 euros. (najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

0630 ET - President Trump's 100% tariff on chip imports could have a severe impact on many Asian economies, HSBC economists say in a research note. Asia accounts for more than 75% of global value-added activities in chip fabrication and roughly up to 85% in assembly, testing, and packaging, they say. Despite the U.S.'s effort to reshore chip manufacturing, it would be "immensely difficult" to replicate Asia's dominance given its advantages in infrastructure, human capital expertise and other factors, the economists say. It's still unclear how much investment is needed for a company to avoid the 100% sectoral tariff, they say. While chip companies will likely announce more investments in the U.S., Asia remains indispensable to the global chip supply chain, they add. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0604 ET - Deliveroo appears to be gaining momentum just as it prepares to the exit the public market, AJ Bell analyst Danni Hewson. The British food-delivery company raised its full-year guidance after a solid first-half performance. With its takeover by U.S. rival DoorDash expected to complete later this year, Deliveroo is showing encouraging signs-- not just in order growth, but more importantly in underlying earnings, margins and cash flow, she says. In May, San Francisco-based DoorDash agreed to acquire Deliveroo for around 2.9 billion pounds in a move to expand its global presence. Shares are down 0.1% at 177.30 pence. (najat.kantouar@wsj.com)United Microelectronics has a 20%-25% exposure to the U.S. market, Citi analysts say. "U.S. Chip Tariffs to Have Limited Impact on Taiwanese Companies — Market Talk," at 0846 GMT, misspelled the company's name.

0526 ET - Xiaomi's 2Q smartphone shipments may come in a bit under Daiwa's prior estimates, weighed by a forecast 25% decline in India. Xiaomi could see mixed smartphone performance across markets for 2Q due to the decline in India, Daiwa analysts say in a note. China market shipments may have retreated to 10.4 million units after two strong quarters, they say. Nevertheless, Daiwa says it's good to see strong smartphone shipments for Xiaomi outside the China and India markets. They project gross profit margin for the smartphone sector to dip to 11.3% as Xiaomi handed out more discounts amid intense competition. Daiwa maintains a buy rating on the stock but cuts its target price to HK$72.00 from HK$78.00. Shares closed at HK$51.85. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0507 ET - Chinese exporters are likely selling more high-value added products to global market, Barclays analysts write in a note. The improvement in July exports appears at odds with the analysts' high-frequency shipping data, which showed some moderation in July. Exports of high value-added products, such as semiconductor, drones, lithium-ion batteries, ships, outperformed low-value added products by a wide margin, Barclays says. Similar divergence was observed in July's PMI and June industrial production data, they say, adding that the strong export performance suggests Chinese exporters are actively seeking alternative buyers and diversifying their markets. The share of exports to non-U.S. markets rose visibly to 89% in July, up from 85% last year, they add. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0504 ET - Apple shares are on the rise Thursday premarket after President Trump said tech companies that promise to manufacture domestically would be exempted from roughly 100% tariffs on all chips coming into the U.S. Apple stock closed 5.1% higher at $213.25 on Wednesday after the iPhone maker pledged a new $100 billion investment in the U.S. Shares are up 2.9% at $219.34 Thursday premarket. Apple's latest investment promise adds to a more than $500 billion commitment over four years the company made back in February. (mauro.orru@wsj.com)

0446 ET - The latest U.S. semiconductor tariffs will likely have minimal impact on Taiwanese chip companies, Citi analysts say in a research note. President Trump said on Wednesday that he would impose roughly 100% tariffs on all chips coming into the U.S., but exempt tech companies that promise to manufacture in the country. Many chips from Taiwan aren't shipped directly to the U.S., instead arriving through downstream assembly and testing partners, they say. United Microelectronics has a 20%-25% exposure to the U.S. market, while Vanguard International Semiconductor's exposure is between 5% and 10%, the analysts say. Both companies' U.S. clients are integrated device manufacturers that handle their own chip production. Other Taiwanese legacy chip makers face limited risks as well, they say. (sherry.qin@wsj.com) Corrections & Amplifications

This article was corrected at 05:57 a.m. ET to fix the spelling of the company name.

0443 ET - Deutsche Telekom shares are likely to be pressured by the slight miss on German service revenue and weaker-than-expected mobile and broadband adds, Berenberg analysts write in a note. In German fixed broadband, net customer adds were negative again in the second quarter, with a loss of 20,000 subscribers compared to consensus expectations of a 5,000 subscriber decline. While the company still targets a flat German broadband base for the year, the analysts see this goal as increasingly challenging given the weak performance. Shares are down 2.9% at 30.40 euros. (najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

0423 ET - Infineon Technologies and STMicroelectronics are likely to be the most affected European chip makers from semiconductor tariffs in the U.S., Citi analysts write in a note to clients. President Trump said he would impose a roughly 100% tariff on chips coming into the U.S. but exempt companies that have promised to manufacture domestically. Analysts note that Infineon recently sold its plant in Texas to SkyWater Technology while STMicroelectronics no longer has a manufacturing presence in the U.S. even though it supplies clients like Elon Musk's Tesla and Apple. Infineon shares trade 1.9% lower at 33.66 euros. STMicroelectronics shares trade 1.4% lower at 21.04 euros. (mauro.orru@wsj.com)

0423 ET - Deutsche Telekom's second-quarter results are disappointing, Citi analysts write in a note. Revenue excluding T-mobile US came in 0.5% below company-compiled consensus, with the German segment missing by 1.7%, they say. The shortfall was driven by weaker equipment sales and a non-cash IFRS 15-related accounting adjustment tied to customer contracts, which also affected Ebitda after leases. Additionally, the soft performance in Germany is likely to raise concerns, they say. The company reported a net loss of 20,000 broadband customers, they add. Shares are down 2.6% at 30.50 euros. (najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

data.title: Health Care Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering the Health Care sector. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1018 ET - Eli Lilly showed its weight-loss treatments are still in high demand, but less-than-stellar results from a study on an experimental anti-obesity pill is what investors are focusing on. The drug maker saw 2Q results jump from a year earlier, prompting it to raise its outlook for the year. Boosting the performance were robust sales for Mounjaro and Zepbound, rising 68% to $5.2 billion and more than doubling to $3.38 billion, respectively. But a study of Eli Lilly's orforglipron, the experimental anti-obesity pill, showed it helped people lose up to about 12% of their body weight after more than a year of treatment, which fell short of analysts' predictions of between 13% and 15% or more. Eli Lilly tumbles 14%. (denny.jacob@wsj.com; @pennedbyden)

0824 ET - Germany's Merck KGaA reported a messy set of second-quarter results, UBS analysts say in a research note. Currency headwinds and one-time charges in its electronics segment offset an encouraging recovery in life science and a solid performance in healthcare, they note. "The key driver of the miss was one-time items in the electronics division which overshadowed continued solid recovery in life science and a strong profit performance at healthcare," the analysts say. The German life science-and-chemical group adjusted its full-year guidance to accommodate those divisional trends, but also raised its expectations of a currency hit and included recent acquisitions and business sales. "Overall, [the group's 2025] targets are broadly unchanged, with the better performing divisions absorbing the drags," they add. Shares fall 0.4%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0513 ET - European luxury companies delivered another sluggish quarter, which prompted worries among many investors around the long-term growth potential of the industry, UBS analysts write in a research note. The limited visibility on third-quarter trends, as well as currency headwinds, seem to have delayed the prospect of a returning earnings momentum after now almost two years of downgrades, the analysts say. UBS expects a sector recovery no sooner than in the second half of 2026. This is due to sector-specific challenges and macroeconomic and political issues, they say. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

0404 ET - Germany's Merck KGaA reported second-quarter results that fell short of expectations at the top and bottom lines due to a weaker performance from the group's electronics segment, Morgan Stanley analysts say in a research note. The German life-sciences and chemicals group reported net sales that were 1% below consensus estimates, and Ebitda before exceptional items that missed forecasts by 4%, the analysts say. Stronger expectations for Merck's life-science and healthcare segments lead the group to raise its full-year guidance for organic growth in adjusted Ebitda, Morgan Stanley says. The revised guidance implies a 2% upgrade to consensus estimates for Merck's full-year adjusted Ebitda, but a 1% cut to sales forecasts, according to Morgan Stanley. Shares fall 3.8%.(adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0304 ET - Sandoz's first-half sales came in slightly ahead of expectations, with growth driven by its biosimilars products and by a strong performance in Europe that offset U.S. weakness, Vontobel's Stefan Schneider says in a research note. "Biosimilars is where the growth is and from a regional perspective, the music plays in Europe--while the U.S. fell short of expectations," Schneider says. The Swiss generic-drug maker reported double-digit sales growth for its biosimilar products--copycat biologic drugs--in the first half and reiterated its full-year guidance. While Sandoz's products are exempted from tariffs for now, its guidance would hold up if it had to live with a 15% tariff on imports from the EU into the U.S., Vontobel says. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

data.title: Financial Services Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Financial Services. Exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

0633 ET - The cost of insuring Swiss banks' credit against default using credit default swaps is unchanged as the 39% tariffs on Swiss imports into the U.S. come into effect. Investors expect the Swiss government to reach a favorable trade deal with the U.S. government. "The strength seen on Thursday for stocks in the region does serve to highlight an optimism that efforts to renegotiate the tariff rate will soon bear fruit," Rostro's Joshua Mahony says in a note. The 5-year credit default swap spreads on Swiss bank UBS Group remain unchanged at 48 basis points, S&P Global Market Intelligence data show. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0537 ET - Raiffeisen Bank International has been delivering improving operating trends and profitability in the past 12 months, UBS says in a research note, raising its rating on the stock to buy from neutral. The Austrian lender's second-quarter results showed that net interest income held up better than expected, fee generation improved and that other income also seems higher, while loan loss provisions remained at muted levels, analysts Mate Nemes and Nicolas O'Sullivan write. Consensus expectations don't appropriately reflect the higher prospective return on tangible equity, they say. Additionally, Raiffeisen could benefit if its planned counter-claim against Russian investment firm Rasperia is successful, they add. UBS also lifts its target share price to 31 euros from 20.6 euros. Shares jump 11% to 27.56 euros. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0508 ET - DBS's increasing dividend payout should set it apart from peers, Citi's Yong Hong Tan says in a note. The Singapore bank's quarterly payout rises to S$0.75 a share, compared with S$0.54 a year ago. DBS has committed to dividend growth and has a visible dividend pathway over 2025-2027, he says. DBS is also continuing its plan to buy back S$3 billion in shares. Tan expects the lender to buy these shares back at a slower pace if the share price stays elevated. Citi raises its target price on DBS to S$56.50 from S$48.10 and maintains its buy rating. Shares are 1.8% higher at S$49.75 after touching an intraday high of S$50. (megan.cheah@wsj.com)

0258 ET - Though Allianz faced headwinds from foreign-exchange fluctuations in the quarter, these didn't affect its print much, JPMorgan says in a research note. The German insurer reported operating profit for the second quarter ahead of expectations on growth in its property and casualty, as well as its life and health insurance divisions. Despite the material impacts of currency moves in the period, assets under management were in line with expectations. This suggests that consensus adjusted quickly to the foreign changes, which were a concern ahead of the results, analysts write. Contractual service margin--a component of an insurer's liabilities--also came in as expected despite being exposed to forex effects, they add. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0246 ET - The weakness in Zurich Insurance's U.S. commercial lines is likely to be in focus, Citi says in a note after the Swiss group's first-half print. The combined operating ratio--a measure of profitability--for the division only advanced by a modest 20 basis points despite strong improvements in some of the turnaround areas, which was offset by deterioration elsewhere, analysts James A. Shuck and Prathamesh Chavan write. This will raise questions around the core operations of that business given that Zurich raised prices in North America. "Elsewhere things look fine and mostly in line, with Retail [gross written premium] growth a highlight and Farmers returning to policy growth with very strong surplus," they add. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0242 ET - There is nothing to complain about in Zurich Insurance's first-half results at first glance, Vontobel says in a research note. The Swiss insurer reported a good set of results that broadly aligned with consensus expectations, analyst Simon Foessmeier writes. Business operating profit rose 6% on year, or 7% in local currencies, illustrating the surprisingly small impact from foreign exchange fluctuations, he says. This is despite the fact that Zurich reports in U.S. dollars, a currency which has weakened in the period. "Given the high expectations, these results will unlikely move the shares much," he adds. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

1849 ET [Dow Jones]--Private fund manager TPG raised $11.3 billion during the 2Q, including $5.4 billion for credit strategies, nearly 80% more than in the same period of 2024 and the second- highest fundraising quarter in the Fort Worth-based firm's history. TPG expects to raise "significantly" more capital this year than in 2024, despite the fundraising hurdles the overall industry still faces, CEO Jon Winkelried says during an earnings call. Investors "remain cautious and highly selective amidst ongoing macro uncertainty and muted distributions," he says, referring to the difficulty that buyout firms in general are having to sell assets and return capital to investors. He notes, however, that TPG generated $6.5 billion in asset sales in 2Q, 20% more than a year earlier. (luis.garcia@wsj.com)

1828 ET - NZX's newest bear says the market is overestimating its sustainable growth level. Forsyth Barr downgrades NZX to underperform from neutral, noting the operator of New Zealand's stock exchange is trading at a higher multiple than its pre-Covid average. It says increased competition in the low-fee passive funds industry has heightened fee pressure and softened net inflows to NZX's high-value KiwiSaver product. It also reckons the headwinds to earnings growth in NZX's capital markets segment in the past decade will persist rather than ease. "Over the last five years, NZX's Ebitda mix has shifted from 90% markets to 40% funds management," analyst Benjamin Crozier says. "With funds management generating lower returns, more cyclical earnings, and higher competitive pressures, it represents a lower-multiple earnings stream." (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

data.title: Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Energy and Utilities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

0920 ET - Oil futures are modestly higher after a five-session losing streak driven by OPEC+ plans to raise output and concerns that U.S. tariffs, which take effect today for dozens of countries, will eventually crimp demand. Market views differ on whether India will or won't stop buying Russian crude, although the additional 25% U.S. tariff is still weeks away and Russia says Presidents Trump and Putin are expected to meet in coming days. Additional increases in OPEC production are the "overriding negative consideration," Ritterbusch says in a note. WTI is up 0.3% at $64.56 a barrel and Brent is 0.3% higher at $67.12. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

0800 ET - Canadian Natural Resources' 2Q performance was as straight down the fairway as could be hoped for, and it continues to deliver, Raymond James' Michael Barth says. As such he view the quarter as neutral, with production in line with his and the consensus forecast and a 3% beat on adjusted funds from operations. Raymond James has an outperform call on the shares, which were last at C$43.12, down 2.8% year-to-date. (robb.stewart@wsj.com)

0517 ET - BP's fortunes are starting turn around after the energy giant delivered significantly stronger results along with positive news about a potential discovery in Brazil's Bumerangue field, Berenberg analysts write. They upgrade their rating on the stock to buy from hold and raise its target price to 500 pence from 385 pence. The oil major's outlook has been boosted by stronger free cash flow after it cut costs and lowered capital expenditure, they add. The incoming chairman will also review costs further while examining its portfolio. As net debt falls, BP should continue attractive buybacks in 2026, they add. Shares trade down 0.3% at 428.95 pence.(adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

1501 ET - Oil futures extend their losing streak to five sessions as the market shrugs off higher U.S. tariffs against India for buying Russian crude oil and a draw in U.S. crude stocks. The additional 25% tariff on Indian goods is due to start in 21 days, which "allows some wiggle room for three-way negotiations between Russia, India, and the U.S. that could possibly keep the oil flowing," Mizuho's Robert Yawger says in a note. OPEC+ output increases and the possible impact of U.S. tariffs on demand remain a drag on prices. The EIA reported a 3 million barrel drop in U.S. crude stocks, with production and imports down on the week, exports up, and refineries running near flat out at 96.9% of capacity. "All the crude oil moving pieces were bullish in today's report," Yawger adds. WTI settles down 1.2% at $65.35 a barrel and Brent falls 1.1% to $66.89. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

data.title: Basic Materials Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Basic Materials. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1023 ET - The agriculture economy remains mixed, Corteva executives say. Low corn and soybean prices are hurting farmer profits, but growers are still shelling out for top-tier crop seed and pesticide products from Corteva to try and maximize their harvests. The company posts increased sales in its seed and pesticide sales in North America for its latest quarter. To boost farmer's fortunes, Corteva CEO Chuck Magro says increased China buying, new trade routes and more crops being used in biofuels are needed. "The market is certainly expecting a record harvest in the U.S.," Magro says on an analyst call. "We're getting close to harvest here in North America and opening up global markets to allow for trade of these critically needed crops would help American farmers continue to feed the world." (patrick.thomas@wsj.com)

2210 ET - Malaysia's wood manufacturing industry is likely to remain under pressure in 2H due to weak global demand, a softer dollar against the ringgit and higher electricity costs, says Hong Leong IB analyst Jack Heng Shao Jun in a note. While tariff uncertainty has eased and resin prices are falling, margin concerns persist amid a challenging outlook, he says. Still, Evergreen Fibreboard stands out for its strong exposure to Middle East construction, particularly Saudi Arabia's Vision 2030, which is driving demand for furniture and wood-based fittings, he notes. Hong Leong maintains a neutral rating on the sector, citing a lack of re-rating catalysts. It pegs Evergreen Fibreboard as its preferred pick. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

1922 ET - BlueScope is a better bet than Sims for Australian steel exposure heading into FY25 results later this month, and more broadly, Jefferies analysts Ramoun Lazar and Kai Erman say in a note. They reckon stronger U.S. spreads should more than offset any effect from softer Australian volumes for steelmaker BlueScope. Lazar and Erman say BlueScope is the only stock in their coverage where Jefferies sits above consensus for 2H FY25 and for FY26. They cite U.S. steel-market tailwinds. Metals recycler Sims remains tethered to scrap prices, which have been rangebound, and trading margins, they say. Their forecasts for Sims are slightly below consensus for 2H FY25, and materially lower for FY26 onwards. "We see little upside to existing volumes without M&A." Jefferies has a "buy" rating on BlueScope, and "underperform" rating on Sims. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

1435 ET - Gold futures snap a 3-day winning streak, inching lower ahead of President Trump's August 7 tariff deadline. Trump has already said Indian imports will be subject to an extra 25% tariff, on top of the 25% rate they already face. Punitive tariffs on Brazil also took effect today, lifting import taxes on some Brazilian goods to 50%. "Today's retreat in gold prices appears to be a result of light profit-taking ahead of the impending end of the U.S. pause on reciprocal tariffs," says Peter Cardillo of Spartan Capital. Front-month gold closed the day down 0.1% to $3,380 a troy ounce. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

data.title: Auto & Transport Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering the Auto and Transport sector. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires at 4:20 ET, 12:20 ET and 16:50 ET.

1437 GMT - Hertz Global's fleet rotation has left it with more than 80% of its U.S. vehicles being less than a year old, CEO Gil West says on a call with analysts. The rental-car company has moved aggressively to swap out the fleet and realign it to better reflect consumer preferences, he says. As a result, depreciation per unit in 2Q hit $251, exceeding Hertz's goal of being below $300. Hertz took early actions to get pre-tariff pricing for its model year 2025 vehicles while selling older vehicles into a frothy market for used cars, the CEO says. The company now has a smaller but higher quality fleet, which should provide for better unit economics going forward, he says. "This approach proved effective, yielding strong cash proceeds and positioning us for more efficient growth," West says. Hertz Global rises 11%.(dean.seal@wsj.com)

1032 GMT - Rheinmetall held back from lifting its medium-term guidance on Thursday but it is still set to raise it once the German government provides some clarity around its defense budget in September or October, mwb research Jens-Peter Rieck tells Dow Jones Newswires. The German arms maker reported a softer second quarter, but its underlying growth story remains clearly intact, he says. The stock's pullback is a good entry point for investors, especially considering the record backlog, the potential for a very strong second half, and the drivers for increased long-term demand, he adds. Free cash flow turned sharply negative, reflecting high capex and inventory build-up ahead of an expected second-half acceleration, Rieck says. Shares are down 5% at 1,694.50 euros. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

0945 GMT - The trade deal between the European Union and the U.S hasn't yet brought any clarity or improvement for the German automotive industry, Hildegard Mueller, president of the German association of the automotive industry, says in a comment. The sectoral tariffs of 27.5% on passenger cars and automotive parts, which have been in force since April and May, respectively, remain in place and are a considerable burden on German car manufacturers and automotive suppliers as well as transatlantic trade, Mueller says. She calls on the U.S. to withdraw the sectoral automotive tariffs. It is important that the relief measures are implemented promptly, she adds. (nina.kienle@wsj.com)

0821 GMT - Cathay Pacific's near-term profitability looks weak, Lorraine Tan, director at Morningstar, writes in a note. Inflationary pressures on staff, passenger and route expenses outweighed savings from lower fuel prices and aircraft charges in 1H, and these cost pressures are expected to persist in the near term, Tan says. Yield normalization was faster than expected, causing a sell-off after the results, and the share price still looks overvalued, Tan says. Morningstar lowers its 2025 estimates for passenger and cargo yields but raises its passenger capacity and load factor forecasts. Morningstar trims its fair value estimate by 9% to HK$9.70. Shares closed 3.0% lower at HK$10.52. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

0815 GMT - Rheinmetall's decision to back its full-year guidance is a conservative move, because its estimates don't yet cover the improvement in market potential that will come from rising global military spending, Morgan Stanley analysts Marie-Ange Riggio and Ross Law say. They expect Germany to convert framework contracts worth 22 billion euros into order intake by the end of the year. Rheinmetall, Germany's biggest arms maker, still has a chance to adjust its forecasts as necessary, they note. "We believe the unchanged guidance seems very much to be a case of conservatism," they write in a research note. The second quarter was soft for orders intake, sales and margin, they add. Shares are down 4.5% at 1,703.50 euros. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

0807 GMT - Maersk's second-quarter results will probably be received strongly by the market, as performance has been significantly better than expected, especially at the earnings level, Jyske Markets senior analyst Haider Anjum writes. All divisions are outperforming, despite geopolitical uncertainty and low visibility in relation to macroeconomic developments, he says. However, it is the guidance upgrade that is perhaps grabbing more of the headlines, with earnings, free cash flow and container volume growth forecasts all lifted, he adds. Maersk's management will hold a conference call later, where Jyske will keep an eye on wording around the consequences of import duties to the U.S. and expectations for 2026 and beyond. Shares in the Danish shipping company rise 4.5%. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0755 GMT - Maersk's second-quarter results came in ahead of expectations, providing a tailwind into the second half on top of resilient demand and prompting management to increase 2025 guidance, JPMorgan analysts write. "Given the feedback from investors in recent days we think the market had expected an upgrade to expectations, and given this setup we do not expect a strong reaction to the shares [Thursday] morning, albeit likely still positive." The bank expects some near-term upgrades to 2025 consensus, but it still expects risks for 2026. The bank expects focus on the management call will be on the near-term outlook and expectations heading into 2026. Shares in the Danish shipping company rise 4.6%. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0739 GMT - Maersk's consensus earnings expectations could be revised modestly higher after second-quarter results beat expectations and full-year guidance was raised, Morgan Stanley's Cedar Ekblom and Peter Ajose-Adeogun write. The main ocean unit saw slightly higher loaded freight rates, better volumes and lower bunker consumption, while the logistics business posted better margins on cost control and commercial focus. In terminals, the company posted strong volumes and utilization rates at near capacity. The bank says it thinks the guidance upgrade might have been largely expected, considering firmer freight rates over the quarter linked to trade volatility and the need to reorganize capacity. A good quarter, which is already reflected in shares, the bank says. Shares rise 3.9%. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0701 GMT - Rheinmetall's shares might underperform Thursday after a weak second quarter, but the longer-term outlook of the German defense company remains strong, Berenberg analysts George McWhirter and Lavinia Norton say. The outlook is still the primary driver of the shares, they note. Rheinmetall is bracing for a significant acceleration in order intake in its defense business, they add. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

0657 GMT - Rheinmetall's second-quarter results were softer than anticipated, but the German defense group backed its full-year guidance and reiterated its expectations for a strong fourth quarter intake from Germany, Jefferies analysts Chloe Lemarie and Ben Brown say. Second-quarter sales of 2.43 billion euros were 4% below consensus, they note. The company's electronic solutions unit is showing weaker margins on year, they add. The main weak point of the release is the free cash outflow of 911 million euros, the analysts say. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

0651 GMT - Rheinmetall's second-quarter results were weak, but that was expected, JPMorgan analysts David Perry and Lucy Fitzgerald say. The German arms maker posted a free cash outflow of 911 million euros, which was 661 million euros lower than they expected. Although this figure doesn't look good, it is rather irrelevant to the investment case, they add. Germany didn't have a government for much of the second quarter, which delayed orders, the analysts note. Rheinmetall spent those months investing in growth and should reap the benefits in the second half of the year and beyond, the analysts say. The group should raise its medium-term guidance meaningfully later this year, they say. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

2254 GMT - This earnings season investors are likely to be relatively unforgiving toward New Zealand companies that qualify any improvement in earnings on an economic recovery or house price inflation, Forsyth Barr says. "After three years, companies have had the time to right size their businesses," analyst Aaron Ibbotson says. Forsyth Barr says Spark NZ, Freightways and Fletcher Building--as well as consumer discretionary companies--should give a good read on New Zealand's economy. "Our Pulse of NZ Survey, published in June 2025, suggested that outside of retail and construction, a painfully slow but steady recovery is taking place and that corporates expect this to continue," Forsyth Barr says. It expects cautiously optimistic outlook statements, with a focus on cost control, conservative balance sheet settings and operational improvement. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

data.title: UnitedHealth to sell off 164 locations to settle US challenge to Amedisys deal
data.description:
UnitedHealth Group (UH.N) has agreed to sell off 164 hospice and home health locations to resolve U.S. antitrust concerns over its $3.3 billion acquisition of Amedisys , the U.S. Justice Department said on Thursday.

The Justice Department and attorneys general from four states sued last year to block the deal, arguing it would reduce competition in the home health services market.

The deal is the latest to be cleared by Trump administration antitrust enforcers, who have shown greater willingness to clear deals than their predecessors in the Biden administration.

"This settlement protects quality and price competition for hundreds of thousands of vulnerable patients and wage competition for thousands of nurses,” said Justice Department antitrust head Gail Slater.

A spokesperson for Optum, UnitedHealth's healthcare provider unit, said in a statement that "with Amedisys, we look forward to continuing meaningful improvements in the home health and hospice care space, a vital part of our value-based care approach."

The Justice Department also fined UnitedHealth $1.1 million for failing to flag missing information during the merger review.

data.title: Mexico Posts Record Light Vehicle Production in July
data.description:
By Anthony Harrup

MEXICO CITY--Mexico's light vehicle production rose in July from a year earlier to its highest level on record for the month and for the first seven months of the year, auto industry association AMIA said Thursday.

Production of cars and light trucks rose 2.4% from July 2024 to 309,453 units, statistics institute Inegi reported. Light vehicle exports were 7.9% higher at 289,598 units, while domestic new-car sales, which include imported models, slipped by 0.6% to 124,482 units.

In the first seven months of the year, light vehicle production was up 0.7% at 2.32 million units. Exports were down 1.4% at 1.96 million units, and domestic new-car sales were down 0.3% at 833,826 units.

U.S. tariffs on auto imports have had little visible impact on domestic production given the exemptions under the U.S.-Mexico-Canada Agreement and the close integration of the North American auto industry. U.S. content in vehicles is subtracted from tariffs and USMCA-compliant parts are excluded.

Mexican auto industry association AMIA said July production and exports were the highest on record for the month, and production the highest ever for the January-July period. "The performance of the automotive industry during the first seven months of the year has kept a good pace despite the challenges posed by the current international context.," AMIA said.

Write to Anthony Harrup at anthony.harrup@wsj.com

Notes:

1.

Rule 8 of the Takeover Code (the "Code") sets out the circumstances in which Dealing Disclosures and/or Opening Position Disclosures are required to be made by the parties to an offer, persons acting in concert with them, persons with an interest in relevant securities of 1% or more and exempt principal traders ("EPTs") connected with the parties to an offer.

2.

Where an offeror is marked "Disclosure of dealings and positions in this offeror is not required", dealings and positions in relevant securities of that offeror are not required to be disclosed under Rule 8. This is either because it has been announced that the offer or possible offer is, or is likely to be, solely in cash or because disclosure is not required for some other reason.

3.

Dealings and positions required to be disclosed under Rule 8 of the Code include dealings and positions in derivatives referenced to, and options in respect of, relevant securities of an offeree company and, where appropriate, an offeror (see Note 2 above).

4.

The information set out in the Disclosure Table relating to the relevant securities in issue of an offeree company or of an offeror (and any amendments thereto) has been extracted from the relevant announcement made by such company under Rule 2.9 of the Code. Similarly, details of any deadline set under Rule 2.6 by which a potential offeror must announce either a firm intention to make a bid or that it does not intend to make an offer (or, in the case of Rule 2.6(a), by which the Panel must consent to an extension of the deadline) has been extracted from the relevant announcement made by the offeree company.

5.

Additions and deletions of offeree companies and offerors are reflected in the Disclosure Table throughout the day, following the relevant announcement. Other amendments may also be made throughout the day. In any event, details of the classes of relevant securities issued by an offeree or offeror company, the number of such securities in issue ("NSI") and the International Securities Identification Number ("ISIN") of such securities announced under Rule 2.9 of the Code before 5.00 pm each day will be included in the final version of the Disclosure Table by 5.30 pm that day. The final version of the Disclosure Table issued each day will be marked "Final" and persons required to make Dealing Disclosures and Opening Position Disclosures are advised to use that final version. Any changes to the Rule 2.6 deadline of a potential offeror will also be reflected in the "Final" version of the Disclosure Table, although any such changes will not be shown in the "Amendments" section at the top of the Disclosure Table.

6.

Dealing Disclosures by the parties to an offer, persons acting in concert with them and connected EPTs must be made by no later than 12 noon on the business day (as defined in the Code) following the date of the relevant dealing and Dealing Disclosures by persons with an interest in relevant securities of 1% or more must be made by 3.30 pm on the business day following the date of the relevant dealing.

7.

Opening Position Disclosures by the parties to an offer and by connected EPTs must be made by no later than 12 noon on the 10th business day after the commencement of the offer period or the announcement that first identifies an offeror (as the case may be). Where required, Opening Position Disclosures by persons with interests in relevant securities of 1% or more must be made by no later than 3.30 pm on the 10th business day after the commencement of the offer period or the announcement that first identifies an offeror (as the case may be). Details of the time and date at which an offer period commenced and at which an offeror was first identified are set out in the Disclosure Table. In order to calculate the deadline for Opening Position Disclosures, it is therefore necessary to count forward 10 business days from the relevant date.

8.

Opening Position Disclosures and Dealing Disclosures required under Rule 8 of the Code must be made to a Regulatory Information Service ("RIS").

9.

This Note is applicable if it says "See Note 9 below" by the name of the company in the Disclosure Table above.  The Executive has granted the company a dispensation from the requirements under the Code that announcements must be published via a RIS. The company is instead required to publish all announcements on its website. The Executive has also granted a dispensation from Note 3 on Rule 8 of the Code that disclosures made under Rule 8 of the Code must be made to a RIS. Therefore, Opening Position Disclosures and Dealing Disclosures required under Rule 8 of the Code may be made to the company at the address stated in the announcement that commenced the offer period.
